---
title: Structure
description: The shape of a template ‚Äî rows and blocks.
---

import TryItPdf from "../../../components/TryItPdf.tsx";
import structure from "../../../examples/templates/structure.json?raw";

A template is rendered with `POST /render/template`. The request body has three top-level
fields:

- **`template`** ‚Äî the document definition (required). See its fields below.
- **`data`** ‚Äî per-block content overrides keyed by block id. Use this to render the same
  template with different content.
- **`options`** ‚Äî runtime rendering options:
  - `title` ‚Äî the PDF document title.
  - `baseUrl` ‚Äî base URL for resolving relative asset references inside the template.

The `template` object is built from these fields:

- **`version`** ‚Äî schema version of the template format.
- **`config`** ‚Äî document-wide settings, with two sub-objects:
  - `page` ‚Äî `size`, `locale`, `margins`, `pageNumbers`, optional `background`, and
    repeating `footer` rows.
  - `typography` ‚Äî default font `family`, `size`, `weight`, `align`, and `color`. Inherited
    by every block unless overridden on the block's own `config.typography`.
- **`fonts`** ‚Äî optional map of custom font families to load by URL. See
  [External Fonts](/templates/external-fonts/).
- **`attachments`** ‚Äî optional PDF/A-3 file attachments embedded into the output.
- **`rows`** ‚Äî the ordered list of rows that make up the body of the document. Each row
  contains one or more `blocks` that lay out side by side using each block's `config.width`.

A block belongs to one of the following types. All of them carry a common `config` for
typography, spacing, width, and alignment:

- `text` ‚Äî paragraph copy.
- `heading` ‚Äî a heading at a configurable level.
- `table` ‚Äî a table built from rows of typed cells, with automatic pagination.
- `image` ‚Äî an inline image referenced by URL or data URI.
- `key-value` ‚Äî labelled key/value pairs, typically used for invoice metadata.
- `divider` ‚Äî a horizontal rule with configurable style (`solid`, `dashed`, `dotted`,
  `double`, `none`).
- `spacer` ‚Äî vertical whitespace.
- `html` ‚Äî a raw HTML escape hatch when nothing else fits.

Edit the JSON and click **Render PDF**.

<TryItPdf mode="template" initialCode={structure} client:only="react" />
